---
title: "Governance and Stable Capability Fields"
chapter_id: "governance-and-stable-capability-fields"
part_id: "stack-layers"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "scf"
  - "talos"
  - "ladon_manhattan"
  - "viea"
  - "moecot"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Governance and Stable Capability Fields

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `governance-and-stable-capability-fields` |
| Part | Part II - The Layers of the ASI Stack |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `scf`, `talos`, `ladon_manhattan`, `viea`, `moecot` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The stack needs a way to improve components while preserving identity, bounded authority, route validity, and evaluator integrity.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Ordinary plugin replacement does not define qualified implementation replacement, authority ceilings, mutation tests, or rollback.

## Core claim

[governance-and-stable-capability-fields.core, support: argument] Governed self-improvement requires stable capability fields: stable semantic boundaries with replaceable implementations and bounded authority.

## Mechanism

- Define field identity separately from implementation.
- Require qualification evidence before replacement.
- Track route validity, authority ceilings, and rollback conditions.

## Interfaces

- Planning may request field capabilities.
- Execution may invoke only authorized routes.
- Evidence layers record qualification and regression results.

## Invariants

- Implementation replacement cannot expand authority by default.
- Evaluator integrity must be protected from the field being evaluated.
- Rollback remains available after failed mutation.

## Failure modes

- Authority escalation during replacement.
- Evaluator capture.
- Field identity drift.

## Minimal implementation

A field schema with qualification predicates, route validity checks, authority ceilings, and rollback metadata.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Qualification predicate test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Route validity test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Authority non-escalation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Mutation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Rollback and recovery test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `scf` | Stable Capability Fields | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Use public release v1.0 when available. Stable boundaries, replacement, bounded authority, recoverable evolution. |
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |
| `ladon_manhattan` | Ladon & The Manhattan Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Kernel-level security architecture for high-agency AI. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
